いやいやいや　そりゃ～ないでしょトレちゃんさー

translate 5 options english informal

1. No way, that's just not right, Toré-chan.
2. Oh, come on, that can't be, Toré-chan.
3. Nah, nah, nah, that's not happening, Toré-chan.
4. Hold on, that's not cool, Toré-chan.
5. Seriously? That's not on, Toré-chan.

浮気しそうな性格してるのはどっちかというとトレちゃんじゃん

translate 5 options english informal

1. If we're talking about who's more likely to cheat, it's gotta be Toré-chan, right?
2. When it comes to a cheating nature, it seems more like Toré-chan, doesn't it?
3. If anyone's got a personality prone to cheating, it's probably Toré-chan.
4. Toré-chan's the one who seems more like the cheating type, if you ask me.
5. Honestly, if we're pointing fingers, Toré-chan's the one who’d likely cheat, you know?

わかんないな～こんな一途なウマ娘　捕まえといて

translate 5 options english informal

1. I dunno, holding onto such a devoted racehorse girl like me.
2. Seriously? You've got a girl this faithful and you still don't get it?
3. How can you not know when you've caught yourself such a loyal horse girl?
4. Man, you don’t see? You’ve snagged yourself a horse girl as steadfast as me.
5. What's not to get? You've got a one-track-mind racehorse girl right here.

そういうとこやぞホントに

translate 5 options english informal

1. That's exactly the kind of thing I'm talking about, for real.
2. That right there is what I mean, seriously.
3. Yep, it's stuff like that, truly.
4. See, that's the issue, honestly.
5. That's precisely it, no kidding.

いやいやいや　そりゃ～ないでしょトレちゃんさー 浮気しそうな性格してるのはどっちかというとトレちゃんじゃん わかんないな～こんな一途なウマ娘　捕まえといて そういうとこやぞホントに

translate 5 options english informal

The given text seems to be a snippet of a conversation with a playful and slightly teasing tone. Here are 5 informal English translations maintaining that tone:

1. No way, no way, that's just not gonna fly, Toré-chan! If anyone looks like they'd cheat, it's totally you, Toré-chan. And you don't even realize you've got a girl as dedicated as me hanging on? That's the real problem, for real.

2. Come on, come on, that's not cool, Toré-chan! Seriously, if I had to guess who'd be the cheater, it'd be you, Toré-chan, right? You're clueless, keeping a one-of-a-kind loyal racehorse girl like me. It's exactly that attitude, honestly.

3. Nah, nah, nah, that can't be right, Toré-chan! It's more like you're the one with a cheating vibe, don't you think, Toré-chan? Don't you get it? You've got such a faithful horse girl right here. And it's definitely that way of yours, truly.

4. Hold up, that's just not on, Toré-chan! When it comes down to it, it looks like you're the one more likely to play around, right, Toré-chan? You don't get it – you've managed to catch a horse girl who's all in. It's really that behavior that's the issue.

5. Seriously? That's totally out of line, Toré-chan! If we're talking who's got a cheating personality, it has to be you, Toré-chan. You have no idea, do you – you're holding onto a horse girl who's totally committed. And that's what's up, for sure.

そりゃ～ないでしょトレちゃんさー

translate 5 options english informal

1. Come on, that's not fair, Toré-chan!
2. Seriously? That's just not right, Toré-chan!
3. No way, that can't be, right, Toré-chan?
4. Dude, that's out of line, Toré-chan!
5. Really now, that's not cool, Toré-chan!

わかんないな～こんな一途なウマ娘　捕まえといて そういうとこやぞホントに

translate 5 options english informal

1. How can you not know? You've snagged yourself such a devoted horse girl, seriously.
2. Can't believe you don't get it – you've got a horse girl who's all about loyalty, for real.
3. Don't you see? You're with a horse girl who's as faithful as they come, honestly.
4. You're clueless, hanging onto a horse girl this dedicated? That's the real deal.
5. You're missing the point – you've caught a horse girl who's totally true-blue, no kidding.

そういうとこやぞホントに

translate 5 options english informal

1. That's exactly what I'm talking about, seriously.
2. It's precisely that, for real.
3. That's the thing I mean, honestly.
4. Yep, that's the spot, truly.
5. That attitude right there is the issue, no joke.

Seems to me it just hasn’t clicked for you how much of a devoted horse girl i am

improve sentence
It appears you haven't quite grasped just how committed and loyal a horse girl I truly am.

Seems to me it just hasn’t clicked for you how much of a devoted horse girl i am

improve grammar
It seems to me that you have yet to understand the extent of my devotion as a horse girl.

Seems to me it just hasn’t clicked for you how much of a devoted horse girl i am

improve grammar 10 options informal

1. Seems like you still don't get how devoted a horse girl I am.
2. You don't seem to realize just how dedicated a horse girl I really am.
3. Looks like it hasn't hit you yet, just how loyal a horse girl I can be.
4. Guess you haven't figured out the level of devotion I have as a horse girl.
5. Feels like you're not seeing just how true-blue of a horse girl I am.
6. I get the impression you haven't quite grasped my commitment as a horse girl.
7. You seem oblivious to the fact that I'm a super devoted horse girl.
8. It hasn't dawned on you yet—the sheer depth of my loyalty as a horse girl.
9. I'm starting to think you haven't noticed just how steadfast a horse girl I am.
10. It's like you can't quite wrap your head around my dedication as a horse girl.

トランセンドさん、めちゃモテそうなのでスカウト成功後も気が気ではない

translate 5 options english

1. Considering how popular Transcend-san seems, I can't help but feel uneasy even after a successful scout.
2. Given Transcend-san's apparent charm, I'm still on edge even after managing to scout him.
3. Transcend-san seems like such a catch; no wonder I'm not at ease despite a successful scouting.
4. With Transcend-san being so likely to attract others, it's no surprise I'm still nervous after scouting him successfully.
5. Since Transcend-san has all the makings of a heartthrob, I remain anxious even after he's been successfully scouted.

Seems to me it just hasn’t clicked for you how much of a devoted horse girl i am

Does this sentence make sense

Yes, the sentence "Seems to me it just hasn’t clicked for you how much of a devoted horse girl I am" makes sense in English. It suggests that the speaker believes the listener has not yet understood or recognized the depth of the speaker's dedication or loyalty, presumably within the context of being a horse girl, which likely refers to their passion for equestrian activities or commitment to their role as a character (possibly in a game or show). The sentence conveys this in an informal and conversational tone.
